p: LogicSplitter is a macOS utility for splitting audio into stems and generating polyphonic WAV files with FCPXML for Final Cut Pro workflows.

This is the first release of LogicSplitter. Woohoo!
To uninstall LogicSplitter, simply drag the application to the trash/bin.
LogicSplitter has been created by Sam Pluemacher.
Special thanks to Knut Hake!
This product uses LateNite's FCPXMLKit for FCPXML processing, created by David Peterson.
The LogicSplitter icon was designed by the amazing Matthew Skiles.
You can learn more about LateNite's creations over at FCP Cafe.
This is the privacy policy for the LogicSplitter, macOS Application that is available on the macOS App Store and TestFlight.
LogicSplitter does not collect any user data. No personal information is transmitted, stored, or used by this application.
LogicSplitter does not use any third-party analytics or tracking tools. It does not contain any in-app purchases or advertisements.
Apple may collect and use certain usage and performance data from the apps that are available on the App Store. This data is used to help improve the App Store and the overall user experience.
This data collection is not specific to any individual user, and it is not possible for app developers to opt out of this data collection by Apple.
You can read Apple's App Store Privacy Policy here.
